sections.heading: What is an Intent?
sections.content: An Intent is a messaging object used to request an action from another Android component. It is mainly used to navigate between Activities, start Services, send Broadcasts, or open features from other apps.

sections.heading: What Intents Can Do
items: Open other apps like browser, camera, dialer

rows.implicit: Action handled by any compatible app
Open browser, share data, dial number

sections.heading: Explicit Intent
sections.content: Explicit Intents specify the exact component to start. They are commonly used to navigate between screens in the same app.

sections.heading: Using Explicit Intent
items: Create intent with target Activity class

sections.heading: Implicit Intent
sections.content: Implicit Intents do not specify a target component. Instead, they declare an action and Android chooses an app capable of handling it.

rows.example: Open a webpage or map location

sections.heading: Intent Flags
items: FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK – start Activity in a new task
FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP – reuse Activity if already on top
FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_HISTORY – Activity not saved in back stack

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Use explicit intents for internal navigation
Check resolveActivity() before starting implicit intents
Use Activity Result API instead of startActivityForResult
Prefer passing IDs instead of large objects

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Intents enable communication between Android components. Explicit Intents handle in-app navigation, while Implicit Intents allow interaction with other apps and system features.
